description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular solar panel mounted on a sturdy frame. The solar panel is elevated above a base structure that appears to house electrical components, possibly including an inverter and battery storage. The base is detailed with a combination of metallic and colored elements, including visible red and black sections that may indicate electrical terminals or control modules. The mounting frame is constructed from metal, providing stability and support for the solar panel at a fixed tilt. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for efficient solar energy capture and integration into urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or rooftops. The object is relatively small, suggesting it is intended for localized power generation or demonstration purposes.
description: A compact solar panel unit with a metal frame and a detailed base housing electrical components, designed for urban solar energy capture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel and the detailed base with colored components.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panel and the layered structure of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel and the supporting frame structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the panel tilt and the arrangement of base components from a different angle.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum) frame
attribute_array_configuration: single rectangular panel mounted on a base
